blocks.content: Clump, seated at the head of the table, had his arms crossed and wore a serious expression.
Next to him sat Kuan, who had come from the swordsmanship school at Clump's summons.
Rian, standing before them, kept his back straight and met Clump's gaze without wavering before speaking.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: This is my decision. It's strange to ask for permission for something I've decided, but please accept it.
Clump let out a deep sigh.
The blue-haired lineage was known for being free-spirited and hot-tempered.
He roughly understood how much Rian had struggled and could empathize with his frustration at not being able to use Schema.
But it had only been a year. It was too early to judge the success or failure of his studies.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: ...Are you running away? What do you plan to do after dropping out of school? Are you thinking of returning to the family for personal training?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: No. I'm going on a knight's quest.
Clump's jaw tightened, causing his beard to twitch.
A mix of anger and exhilaration surged within him.
How could Rian think so much like he did when he was young?
Even so, he had to oppose it.
When Clump was young, many embarked on knightly quests.
The world was in turmoil, and unifying it with the sword wasn't just a dream.
But times had changed.
The sword was a tool for killing.
While real combat was the best training, it was a double-edged sword that could end one's life with a single mistake.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: I won't say you're too young. You're almost nineteen. But can you truly say there's nothing left to learn at school? Is the school really that insignificant to you?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: I've never thought of it that way. I just want to become stronger.
This time, Clump also showed his anger.
Being pleased that Rian resembled him was one thing, but following the same path as his predecessors was another.
Above all, he couldn't push his beloved grandson into danger.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: You want to leave on a quest to become stronger? Do you think you can get away with such childishness?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: It's not childishness! I'm serious about risking my life...
Clump was firm.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: No means no. You can experience real combat when the time comes. First, you need to build enough skill to face the world.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: No. I've already submitted my withdrawal. The decision is made.
I canceled it. This old man, nearing seventy, begged your junior to have it rescinded.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Grandfather!

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: It wasn't just about the withdrawal being rescinded; it was frustrating that Clump had to plead with someone on his behalf.
Just as Rian was about to speak out of his burning emotions, there was a knock at the door.
Clump frowned, annoyed.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: What is it? We're in the middle of a conversation. Come back later.

blocks.speaker: Louis
blocks.content: Sir, Shirone is here to see you.
Rian turned to the door in surprise. His heart suddenly began to race.
His closest friend and the lord to whom he had sworn fealty. For whatever reason, he was the one person Rian never wanted to show this side of himself.
Clump also knew what Shirone meant to Rian.
After a moment of thought, he made a decision.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Let him in.
Rian's stubborn personality wouldn't be swayed by family, but Shirone might be able to change the situation.
The door opened, and Shirone entered.
Tess peeked in to gauge the room's atmosphere, but Louis quickly closed the door, giving her no chance.
Rian instinctively avoided Shirone's gaze.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hello, sir.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Yes. Reina told me. You've been through a lot.
He already knew what Shirone had gone through in Kazra.
Shirone's aura had become noticeably stronger after his trials.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Rian dropped out of school.
Clump exaggeratedly scratched his head in frustration.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Yes. Try to persuade him. He's being so stubborn.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I support his decision.
Clump's hand stopped scratching.
Even Kuan, who didn't know Shirone, looked surprised.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Support? Are you saying it's okay for Rian to drop out?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes. I don't know the details or the reasons, but if Rian decided that, I believe it's the right path.
Clump tried to maintain his composure.
He had hoped Shirone's arrival would be a turning point, but if Shirone was going to defend Rian, it would be even more troublesome.
He would be a very difficult advocate.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Alright. As Rian's lord, your opinion is worth considering. But if you think that way, you must have a valid reason.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: There is no valid reason.
What?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I don't know which choice is more efficient for Rian. But I can guarantee one thing.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The Rian I know never runs away from anything.
Rian clenched his fists.
Those words gave him more confidence than a thousand others.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: It might be a foolish decision. But it's not an attempt to avoid anything. As his friend, I believe supporting him is the right thing to do.
Clump couldn't help but laugh, feeling a mix of frustration and admiration.
It was a beautiful friendship.
And it's human nature to want to protect beautiful things.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: Clever boy. He already knows my weak spot.
Dealing with the family head Bishoff was different.
Clump, a man of honor, couldn't firmly oppose such an argument.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: I understand your feelings. And your argument is persuasive. But Shirone, that's just your and Rian's perspective. You two don't know the world yet.

blocks.speaker: Clump
blocks.content: I've walked the path of a swordsman for decades. Do you think it's just about school? Not at all. One day, this dropout will come back to haunt Rian.
Kuan raised his hand.
Everyone turned their attention to Kuan.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: I have something to say.
It was Rian's withdrawal letter.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: I respect and want to help you, sir, but I can't allow the withdrawal to be rescinded. Teaching a student who has already given up on school goes against my educational philosophy.
For Clump, it was one obstacle after another.
Kuan's stubbornness was well-known at the swordsmanship school.
It didn't matter if his opponent was a certified third-grade swordsman general.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Please, just this once. Can't you let it slide?
Rian felt suffocated.
Seeing his grandfather plead with a junior for his sake made him feel his grandfather's love.
But Kuan was not an easy opponent.
As a teacher, it's an irresponsible opinion, but Rian has no future at the school.
There are technical aspects he can learn, but the main evaluation criteria are Schema.
There's a growing opinion that brain enhancement falls under Schema, but it's not something the school can teach.
And practice and real combat are entirely different realms.
Winning a wrestling match among cadets doesn't count as a score.
Kuan's words didn't sound particularly unpleasant.
He was simply giving his honest opinion as someone who had closely observed Rian.
The room fell silent.
No one spoke.

blocks.speaker: Louis
blocks.content: Sir, the second young master has arrived.
Rian's face twisted in anger.
Ozent Rai. Of all times, his lifelong rival had to come home now.
He was someone Rian didn't want to see, just like Shirone, but for different reasons.
As Rai entered confidently, Shirone quickly stepped aside.
He had only seen Rai once when he worked as the Ozent family's librarian, but his impression was vivid.
Rai had black hair and a cold face.
His demeanor was still icy, but his presence had grown significantly stronger.
When Reina followed him in, even Louis couldn't close the door this time.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Hey! Why are you so busy that you don't even show your face? Do you know it's been a month since you came home?
Even the cold Rai couldn't help but frown at his sister's nagging.

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: I was busy. I couldn't help it. I'm here to pack for a deployment soon. I'm hungry, so make me some food.
Reina, fuming, glared at the back of Rai's head before whispering to Louis to prepare a meal.
After all, her brother had come home after a long time, so she wanted to send him off with a warm meal.
Rai approached Clump and bowed formally.
Now that he was also a soldier, he couldn't be casual even at home.

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: Grandfather.
Hmm, you seem to be doing well. Ah, introduce yourself. This is Kuan, a teacher at the swordsmanship school.
Rai's eyes changed.
Kuan? The Death Magician Kuan?
After passing the certification exam and joining the military, he had heard stories.
Although unconfirmed, the only swordsman who had survived a battle with Feng Zhang was said to be Kuan.
Rai turned to Kuan and bowed.

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: Hello. I'm Ozent Rai.

blocks.speaker: Kuan
blocks.content: Yes, I've heard about you. You have a good disposition.
Kuan received the greeting as politely as possible, showing a different attitude compared to when he dealt with Rian.
So this is the genius swordsman of the Ozent family.
Last year, he had achieved the 10th rank certification, and now he was already an 8th rank sergeant.
His aura was cold and sharp, reminiscent of Kuan's younger days.
Rai stood at attention, allowing Kuan to fully assess him, then turned back to Clump and asked.

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: But why is a high-ranking official from the swordsmanship school here?

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Rian dropped out of school. Grandfather called him to try to change his mind.

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: Dropped out of school?
Rai turned to Rian to confirm, and Rian glared back.
He didn't want to lose even a staring contest with Rai, unlike with Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: What's the big deal? If he wants to quit, let him quit.
Reina clicked her tongue in disapproval.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: How can you be so indifferent? Aren't you worried about your brother's future?
Rai walked past Rian towards the door.

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: He was never going to make it. It's better to give up now. Hurry up and get me some food. I'm hungry.
As Rian followed Rai with his eyes, he spoke up.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Wait a minute.
When Rai turned around, Rian pointed a finger at his forehead.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: You didn't go to school either.

blocks.speaker: Rai
blocks.content: That's different. I don't need to, and for you, it's a waste of time.
Rian's teeth ground together in frustration.
